rate_analysis.rate_verdict: REVERSED — rate increased from 0.0024 to 0.00356 (NOT continued deceleration)

equilibrium_analysis.verdict: ABOVE equilibrium — S397 principle-batch (P-255→P-264) likely driver. High-citation batches cause rate spikes.
equilibrium_analysis.K_avg_equilibrium_projection: 4.5 at N→∞ still holds, but path is non-monotone

hub_trajectory.hub_citation_rate: 17/21 = 81% of new lessons cite top hub — extremely high
hub_trajectory.interpretation: Super-linear PA accelerating. Top hub accumulating citations at 81% new-lesson rate.

key_findings: K_avg rate reversal: 0.0024 (S393-S396) → 0.00356 (S396-S398). Deceleration not monotone.
Hub z acceleration: 26.8 → 34.9. Top hub gained 17 in-citations in 21 lessons (81% rate).
Edges-per-new-lesson 5.24 — above equilibrium target 4.5. Principle-batch effect likely.
K_avg 2.6617 confirms long-run equilibrium trajectory toward 4.5. Not there yet.
Rate reversals may be driven by batch events (principle scans, compaction sessions that re-cite).
lesson_candidate: Rate reversal mechanism: batch-citation events (principle scans, lesson batch commits) create spikes in edges-per-lesson that temporarily accelerate K_avg growth. L-801 equilibrium is the long-run attractor, not a monotone convergence path.
verdict: F-NK5 tracking at N=745. CONFIRMED: K_avg ~2.6, Hub z >26. FALSIFIED: rate deceleration (reversed). NEW: Hub gaining citations at 81% new-lesson rate. Batch events drive non-monotone K_avg path.
